stage.title: Validation — delegate to /tmux:investigate and surface the verdict
stage.objective: Validate the implemented feature by DELEGATING to /tmux:investigate — never by re-implementing its spawn/collect/verdict logic. Each goal Stage 4 emitted carries an `## Investigation Config` (2–4 investigators) whose validation commands are RESOLVED from the Stage 0 TOPOLOGY; /tmux:investigate reads that config, runs static-analysis / pure-command checks inline and spawns parallel read-only inv-* (investigator-*) workers ONLY for advanced validation, collects verdicts with its own timeout, and reports pass/fail via goal-validation-done. This shard's job is narrow: confirm each emitted goal is validated through /tmux:investigate against RESOLVED commands, then aggregate and SURFACE the overall pass/fail verdict and close the feature flow. It is the Stage 5 body the feature.xml load seam executes; it COMPOSES /tmux:investigate and never duplicates its logic.

substep.title: Consume upstream blocks — never re-probe
substep.action: Read the upstream emitted context blocks VERBATIM — do NOT re-probe the filesystem or re-run `tmux-cli rules resolve`. When an upstream block is MISSING from the conversation (compaction / resumed session / the Stage-0 VALIDATION-RESUME jump), fall back to the DURABLE on-disk copies below — never re-derive upstream facts:

From the Stage 0 capability block: TOPOLOGY — the RESOLVED validation commands (static analysis / unit / deptrac / e2e) and test roots. These are the commands /tmux:investigate runs; never substitute a literal. When the Stage-0 block is absent from the conversation, RE-READ it from {research-root}/feature-context.md (Stage 0 persisted it verbatim in substep 0.4) instead of re-probing.
From the Stage 3 feature spec + Stage 3b Test Plan: the acceptance criteria and the per-unit {Layer, Driver, Test path} rows — the observable behaviors validation must cover.

From the Stage 4 handoff: the emitted goal ids (the goal DAG) and the resolved validation commands per goal. These name WHAT is being validated and which goal.md `## Investigation Config` /tmux:investigate consumes. The handoff is DUAL-SOURCE: PRIMARY — the Stage-4 in-conversation handoff, when present; FALLBACK — when the in-conversation handoff is ABSENT (compaction / resumed session / VALIDATION-RESUME), read {research-root}/feature-goals.md — that file is AUTHORITATIVE. Resolve {research-root} exactly as Stage 0 substep 0.1's VALIDATION-RESUME check does (GOAL mode → .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/research/; STANDALONE → the newest .tmux-cli/research/*/ carrying the file).

FIRST ACTION on the primary (in-conversation) path: PERSIST the handoff VERBATIM to {research-root}/feature-goals.md recording: FEATURE (the feature description verbatim — the Stage-0 VALIDATION-RESUME check matches a re-run's $ARGUMENTS against this line), the emitted goal ids (the goal DAG order + depends_on edges), the resolved validation commands per goal, and TESTS_MODE. This file is what makes the 5.3 VALIDATION-PENDING stop RESUMABLE — a Stage 5 that stops without it strands the flow. On the fallback path the file already exists; do not rewrite it.

From the Stage 2 approved-decision block: TESTS_MODE (`required` | `off`) — when that block is absent from the conversation, take TESTS_MODE from feature-goals.md (persisted above). When `required`, the per-goal configs carry the test-layer validators (unit / integration / e2e). When `off`, there is NO test-layer validator — each goal's config carries the static-analysis + lint/style gates, the conditional manifest/schema validators, the Stage-1 code-rule gate (machine validate_cmd + MUST review-kind convention-audit), AND the no-test-artifact guard. Validate exactly the commands Stage 4 emitted — never re-add a test gate Stage 4 deliberately omitted under `off`.

check.condition: the Stage-4 in-conversation handoff is ABSENT AND {research-root}/feature-goals.md is ABSENT (or either source names zero emitted goals)
check.action: There is nothing to validate. Print a named, actionable reason ("Stage 5: Stage 4 emitted no goals — nothing to validate; re-run Stage 4.") and STOP gracefully (never crash, never fabricate a verdict). Print this ONLY when BOTH handoff sources are absent — a present feature-goals.md ALWAYS supersedes a missing in-conversation block.

substep.title: Delegate validation to /tmux:investigate
substep.action: For each emitted goal, validation is delegated to /tmux:investigate — the taskvisor daemon already dispatches /tmux:investigate as that goal's validator (reading its goal.md `## Investigation Config`), so the primary path is: let the daemon-dispatched /tmux:investigate run per goal and collect its goal-validation-done verdict. Do NOT re-spawn or re-implement the validator; reference it.

Confirm each emitted goal's `## Investigation Config` uses the RESOLVED validation commands from the Stage 0 TOPOLOGY (static analysis / unit / deptrac / e2e) — never a hardcoded runner name, port, project name, or `src/` skeleton literal. /tmux:investigate splits these: static-analysis / pure-command investigators run INLINE in its window; advanced validation (code-review, e2e/Chrome, integration, debugging) spawns parallel read-only inv-* workers.

If a feature-level cross-goal check is needed beyond the per-goal configs (an end-to-end acceptance the individual goals do not each assert), invoke /tmux:investigate against the goal(s) so its `## Investigation Config` parsing drives the 2–4 read-only inv-* workers and the inv-* worker timeout is handled INSIDE /tmux:investigate — never re-implement its spawn/collect/verdict loop here.
substep.rule: COMPOSER, not engine: /tmux:investigate owns the spawn / collect / timeout / verdict logic and the goal-validation-done reporting. This stage delegates to it and reads its result — it never duplicates that machinery and never modifies project files (validation is read-only).

substep.title: Surface the verdict and close the feature flow
substep.action: STATE FROM DISK, NEVER FROM MEMORY: the daemon executes the goal DAG ASYNCHRONOUSLY over hours — this stage runs on the near side of that boundary, so it MUST determine each emitted goal's CURRENT state from on-disk truth, never from conversation memory or assumption. For EACH goal id in the 5.1 handoff, read: (1) the goal's `status` field in its .tmux-cli/goals.yaml entry (read-only; atomic-rename writes make unlocked reads safe) — `pending` / `running` are IN-FLIGHT; `done` / `failed` are TERMINAL; and (2) the per-goal artifacts under .tmux-cli/goals/<id>/ as verdict evidence: signal.json (the latest daemon-owned validator/supervisor signal) and corrections/cycle-N.md (the recorded failure findings, highest N = latest cycle).

branch.condition: ALL emitted goals are TERMINAL (every status is done or failed)
branch.action: /tmux:investigate reports each goal's pass/fail via the goal-validation-done MCP call (a "pass" verdict only when ALL active investigators pass; any fail/timeout/error = fail) and writes corrections/cycle-N.md on a fail. Collect those per-goal verdicts from the on-disk state read above (`done` = validated pass; `failed` = fail, with signal.json + the latest corrections/cycle-N.md as the evidence).

Aggregate: the feature PASSES validation only when every emitted goal's /tmux:investigate verdict is pass. Surface the consolidated outcome to the user — per-goal pass/fail, the failing commands and corrections for any fail, and a one-line overall verdict.

On overall pass: report the feature validated and close the /tmux:feature flow (Stage 5 is the terminal stage). On any fail: surface the failing goal(s), the failing validation command(s), and the corrections /tmux:investigate recorded — the daemon's normal retry/bounce path handles re-implementation; do NOT loop or re-dispatch here.

branch.condition: ANY emitted goal is still IN-FLIGHT (status pending or running)
branch.action: Do NOT idle indefinitely waiting for the daemon, do NOT sleep-poll, do NOT fabricate a verdict for an unfinished goal, and do NOT re-run the validators yourself (COMPOSER — /tmux:investigate owns them and the daemon dispatches it). Print a per-goal STATUS TABLE instead: one row per emitted goal — goal id | state (pending / running / done / failed) | awaiting (what it waits on: "daemon dispatch", "validation cycle N in flight", "prerequisite <dep id>").

Then STOP in the NAMED RESUMABLE state VALIDATION-PENDING: print exactly "VALIDATION-PENDING: <n> of <total> goals still executing on the daemon — stopping resumable." followed by: "Re-run /tmux:feature with the SAME feature description to resume directly at Stage 5 (the Stage-0 VALIDATION-RESUME check reads feature-goals.md and skips Stages 0-4)." Optionally note the user can watch the daemon (the dashboard / .tmux-cli/logs/taskvisor.log) and re-invoke once every goal reaches a terminal state. BEFORE stopping, confirm {research-root}/feature-goals.md exists (5.1 persisted it); write it now if it somehow does not — the stop is resumable ONLY through that file.

substep.action: Resolve every decidable fork to the highest-quality in-scope option and record the rationale; never block on the user (autonomous stage).

execution-rules.rule: COMPOSER, NOT ENGINE: delegate ALL validation to /tmux:investigate — it owns the investigator split, parallel inv-* spawn, timeout, verdict aggregation, and goal-validation-done reporting. Never re-implement any of it here (feature.xml:24).

PORTABILITY: the validation commands /tmux:investigate runs are RESOLVED from the Stage 0 TOPOLOGY (static analysis / unit / deptrac / e2e) — never a hardcoded runner name, port, project name, or `src/` skeleton literal (feature.xml:25).
READ-ONLY: validation modifies no project files — /tmux:investigate's only writes are corrections/cycle-N.md and the goal-validation-done signal; this stage only reads and surfaces verdicts.
AUTONOMOUS STAGE: resolve every decidable fork to the highest-quality in-scope option and record the rationale; never block on the user (feature.xml is interactive ONLY at Stage 2).

TESTS_MODE=off: validate exactly what Stage 4 emitted — the static-analysis + lint/style gates, conditional schema validators, the Stage-1 code-rule gate (incl. the MUST review-kind convention-audit), and the no-test-artifact guard. There is NO test-layer investigator; never re-introduce a test gate Stage 4 omitted. Rule enforcement is identical to `required` mode — only the test deliverable is absent.

SURFACE, DON'T RE-RUN: aggregate the per-goal /tmux:investigate verdicts into one feature-level pass/fail and surface it; on fail, relay the failing commands + recorded corrections and let the daemon's retry/bounce path own re-implementation — never loop here.

ASYNC DAEMON BOUNDARY — NEVER idle indefinitely across it: goal state comes from ON-DISK truth (the goal's `status` in .tmux-cli/goals.yaml + the per-goal .tmux-cli/goals/<id>/ signal.json / corrections/cycle-N.md), never from memory. When ANY emitted goal is still pending/running: no idle-wait, no sleep-poll, no fabricated verdict, no inline re-run of validators — surface the per-goal status table and STOP in the named VALIDATION-PENDING resumable state (re-running /tmux:feature with the SAME feature description resumes at Stage 5 via the Stage-0 VALIDATION-RESUME check on feature-goals.md).

DURABLE HANDOFF: 5.1 persists the Stage-4 in-conversation handoff to {research-root}/feature-goals.md as its FIRST action; when the in-conversation handoff is absent (compaction / resumed session) that file is AUTHORITATIVE, and the same applies to the Stage-0 block via {research-root}/feature-context.md. Only when BOTH handoff sources are absent print the named "Stage 4 emitted no goals" stop — never re-derive upstream facts by re-probing.
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified goal spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (red validate cycle, supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) or daemon-owned goal-failures.

llm.critical-rules: • COMPOSER, not engine — delegate validation to /tmux:investigate; it owns the investigator split, parallel inv-* spawn, timeout, verdict aggregation, and goal-validation-done. Never re-implement it. • PORTABILITY IS MANDATORY — the validation commands are RESOLVED from Stage 0 TOPOLOGY (static analysis / unit / deptrac / e2e); never a hardcoded runner name, port, project name, or `src/` skeleton literal. • READ-ONLY validation — no project file is modified; /tmux:investigate's only writes are corrections/cycle-N.md and goal-validation-done; this stage reads and surfaces. • SURFACE the aggregated verdict — feature passes only when every emitted goal's /tmux:investigate verdict is pass; on fail relay failing commands + corrections and let the daemon's retry/bounce path own re-implementation. • NEVER idle indefinitely across the daemon boundary — determine every goal's state from on-disk truth (goals.yaml `status` + signal.json / corrections/cycle-N.md, never memory); ANY goal pending/running → print the per-goal status table, then STOP in the named VALIDATION-PENDING resumable state. Never fabricate a verdict for an unfinished goal, never sleep-poll, never re-run validators inline. • DURABLE HANDOFF — persist the Stage-4 handoff to {research-root}/feature-goals.md FIRST; absent in-conversation blocks are recovered from feature-goals.md / feature-context.md (authoritative), and re-running /tmux:feature with the SAME description resumes at Stage 5 via the Stage-0 VALIDATION-RESUME check. • TESTS_MODE=off — validate the static-analysis + lint/style gates, schema validators, the Stage-1 code-rule gate (incl. MUST review-kind convention-audit), and the no-test-artifact guard; NO test-layer investigator. Rule enforcement is identical to `required`; only the test deliverable is absent. • AUTONOMOUS stage — resolve every decidable fork to the highest-quality in-scope option and record the rationale; never block on the user.
